# UC-22.2.5 · NIS2 Network and Information Systems Access Control Audit

## Description

Correlates interactive sign-in success and failure, privilege-use signals, and privileged-session telemetry across Windows domain assets, Entra ID, Okta, and CyberArk vault sessions so reviewers can spot weak logon types, after-hours elevation, and cross-system identity sprawl affecting essential services.

## Value

Regulators and internal audit teams need a defensible narrative that access to network and information systems is logged, attributable, and reviewable without assembling spreadsheets from five admin portals. This pattern concentrates those signals into one scheduled artefact with timestamps suitable for year-end NIS2 evidence packs.

## Implementation

(1) Deploy Splunk Add-on for Windows (742) with Security log collection; enable Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (3110) Graph sign-in inputs; enable Okta (6553) and CyberArk (2891) inputs for `index=auth` / `index=pam`. (2) In `authorize.conf`, grant the compliance role `srchIndexesAllowed` for `windows`, `auth`, `pam`. (3) Maintain `nis2_service_accounts.csv` and `nis2_access_review_exceptions.csv` lookups. (4) Schedule daily; archive PDF/CSV to `index=audit_evidence`. (5) Optional: accelerate the CIM Authentication model for executive roll-ups.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Splunk Enterprise 9.x or Splunk Cloud plus Universal Forwarders on every domain-joined Windows server collecting `WinEventLog:Security` into `index=windows` via Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows ([Splunkbase 742](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742)).
• Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services ([Splunkbase 3110](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3110)) with OAuth stored under **Settings → Passwords** and modular inputs polling Microsoft Graph sign-ins into `index=auth` `sourcetype=azure:aad:signin` (confirm exact sourcetype in **Activity → Data Inputs** after first poll).
• Splunk Add-on for Okta ([Splunkbase 6553](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/6553)) streaming `sourcetype=okta:log` and Splunk Add-on for CyberArk ([Splunkbase 2891](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2891)) landing `index=pam` `sourcetype=cyberark:session`.
• Role `nis2_access_review` granted search rights on `windows`, `auth`, `pam`, and read access to lookups `nis2_service_accounts.csv`, `nis2_access_review_exceptions.csv`, optional `nis2_dc_inventory.csv`.
• Group Policy baseline: Success/Failure auditing for account logon and logon events on Tier-0 systems; align event volume expectations (~2–6 KB/EventCode per interactive user per day on busy admin jump boxes).

Step 1 — Configure data collection
• Windows: enable the Security channel via inputs.conf `winevtlog://Security` with `checkpointInterval=5` and `current_only=0` during bootstrap, then tighten to `current_only=1` once backlog drains (monitor with `index=_internal source=*metrics.log` for lag).
• Graph sign-in input: validate earliest poll is `-24h` while tuning filters to essential services only (reduce license burn). Capture `userPrincipalName`, `ipAddress`, `authenticationRequirement`, `conditionalAccessStatus`.
• CyberArk: confirm each session event contains `session_id`, `user`, `source_host`; map TIME_FORMAT if milliseconds differ from vault defaults.
• Expected latency: Windows ≤90 s, Graph/Okta ≤300 s, CyberArk ≤120 s — document worst case per feed for auditors.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
```spl
(index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode IN (4624, 4625, 4672)) OR (index=auth sourcetype IN ("azure:aad:signin","okta:log")) OR (index=pam sourcetype="cyberark:session") earliest=-24h
| eval user=coalesce(Account_Name, userPrincipalName, user, Username)
| eval auth_result=case(EventCode=4624,"success", EventCode=4625,"failure", EventCode=4672,"special_privileges", match(lower(_raw),"(?i)success"),"success", match(lower(_raw),"(?i)fail|denied"),"failure", 1=1,"other")
| eval after_hours=if(tonumber(strftime(_time,"%H"))<7 OR tonumber(strftime(_time,"%H"))>19, 1, 0)
| stats count by sourcetype, EventCode, auth_result, user, dest, Logon_Type, after_hours
| sort -count
```
Understanding this SPL: the union OR captures network OS, cloud IdP, and PAM in one pass; `coalesce` unifies naming so HR-offboard checks in UC-22.2.14 can `join` on `user`; `auth_result` approximates cloud success/failure when EventCode is null; `after_hours` flags elevated risk windows for SOC triage.
Save as alert **UC-22.2.5 — NIS2 access control audit** with cron `15 */6 * * *`, trigger if `after_hours=1` AND `auth_result IN ("failure","special_privileges")` after post-filter `| where` in a companion alert.

Step 3 — Validate
• Pick one Windows admin: compare latest `EventCode=4624` `_time` to the Security Event Log timestamp on the host (`wevtutil qe Security /q:"*[System[(EventID=4624)]]" /f:text /c:1`).
• For Entra ID, cross-check the same `userPrincipalName` + minute bucket against **Microsoft Entra admin center → Monitoring → Sign-in logs**.
• Compare `sourcetype=cyberark:session` counts to **Privileged Access Security → Sessions → Live** for a 1-hour slice.
• Run `| tstats count WHERE index=windows OR index=auth OR index=pam by index,sourcetype` — investigate any sourcetype with 0 events while onboarding.

Step 4 — Operationalize
• Dashboard row 1: heatmap of `auth_result` × hour-of-day. Row 2: top 20 users by failure count with drilldown to raw events. Row 3: CyberArk-only panel with median session duration.
• Route alert actions to ServiceNow `sn_si_incident` when failure spikes exceed 3σ of the prior 7 days.
• Weekly CSV export signed per `evidenceSigning` and stored under `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:saved_search`.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **No Graph rows** — token expiry or missing `SignIn.Read.All`; reauthorize in Splunk Add-on UI and tail `$SPLUNK_HOME/var/log/splunk/ta_ms_cloud_services.log`.
• **Okta rate limits** — lower poll interval to 300 seconds or switch to streaming HTTP collector.
• **CyberArk session_id null** — upgrade TA 2891 ≥3.x or add `FIELDALIAS` in `props.conf` for your vault build.
• **4625 storms after password spray** — temporarily raise threshold via `where count>20` and open a hunt ticket referencing MITRE T1110.


## SPL

```spl
(index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode IN (4624, 4625, 4672)) OR (index=auth sourcetype IN ("azure:aad:signin","okta:log")) OR (index=pam sourcetype="cyberark:session") earliest=-24h
| eval user=coalesce(Account_Name, userPrincipalName, user, Username)
| eval auth_result=case(EventCode=4624,"success", EventCode=4625,"failure", EventCode=4672,"special_privileges", match(lower(_raw),"(?i)success"),"success", match(lower(_raw),"(?i)fail|denied"),"failure", 1=1,"other")
| eval after_hours=if(tonumber(strftime(_time,"%H"))<7 OR tonumber(strftime(_time,"%H"))>19, 1, 0)
| stats count by sourcetype, EventCode, auth_result, user, dest, Logon_Type, after_hours
| sort -count
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Timechart of failed logons (4625), table of after-hours 4672 events, stacked bar of auth_result by sourcetype, single value of unknown users exceeding threshold.
